title: Relapsed AML with FLT3-ITD in a 35-year-old male

1.capabilities: Immediate assessment of acute physiology. You have access to point-of-care diagnostics, standard laboratory panels, bedside imaging, and emergency cross-sectional imaging (CT). Specialized tissue sampling (biopsies) or molecular diagnostics are not yet available.

2.capabilities: The patient is admitted and stable enough for more detailed or invasive diagnostic procedures. You have access to tissue acquisition, comprehensive laboratory or microbiology workup, more detailed and focused imaging, and immunophenotyping. Long-term culture and molecular results are still pending processing.

3.capabilities: Definitive diagnostic characterization phase. Results from long-turnaround tests are now available. You have access to cytogenetics, molecular panels, and Measurable Residual Disease (MRD) assessments. You may also request data on response to initial therapeutic interventions or prior therapy courses.

demographics.valid_request_if: Requests for demographics and baseline characteristics such as age, sex, ECOG/performance status, relevant comorbidities, social/family history, or anthropometrics (height, weight).
demographics.content:
Age: 35
Sex: Male
Performance status: ECOG 1-2
Comorbidities: Reduced right ventricular function, tricuspid regurgitation, history of pulmonary embolism
Social: Lives alone, both parents alive, no children, history of intravenous drug use, ex-smoker (50 py)
Family history: Maternal grandfather died of lung cancer
Height: 181cm; Bodyweight: 87kg.

history_physical.valid_request_if: Requests for the history of present illness, review of systems, physical examination findings, bedside observations, or the current outpatient medication list.
history_physical.content:
On May 26, 2025, the patient presented to the emergency department with shortness of breath and palpitations. He additionally reported swelling, redness, and hardening of the skin in the left elbow region. Other symptoms included difficulty sleeping, exertional dyspnea, and a mild unintentional weight loss. Chest pain was denied. On examination, the patient appeared pale with scattered petechiae on the legs, hematomas on the forearms, and white lesions on the oral mucosa. Hepatomegaly was noted, while no lymphadenopathy was present. The skin appeared dry, and the left antecubital region showed painful erythema with induration. Bilateral lower leg edema was present, while neurological status was unremarkable.
Current medication: pantoprazole 40 mg 1-0-0, torsemide 5 mg 1-0-0, vitamin D 20.000 IE 1×/week, posaconazole 300 mg 1×/d, beclomethasone/formoterol inhaler p.r.n., dapagliflozin 10 mg 1-0-0.

cbc.valid_request_if: Requests for a complete blood count (CBC/FBC), white blood cell count, hemoglobin/hematocrit, platelet count, RBC indices (MCV/MCH), or a differential including blast percentage.
cbc.content: CBC: Hb 8.3 g/dL, WBC 104 ×10⁹/L, Plt 12 ×10⁹/L; MCV normal, MCH normal; differential: 30% blasts, 60% monocytes, 8% neutrophils, 2% lymphocytes

coagulation.valid_request_if: Requests for coagulation studies such as PT/INR, aPTT, fibrinogen level, D-dimer, or a general coagulation profile.
coagulation.content: Coagulation: aPTT 42 sec, INR 2.1, fibrinogen 2.1 g/L, D-dimer normal

chemistry.valid_request_if: Requests for a chemistry/metabolic panel (CMP), electrolytes, renal or hepatic function tests, tumor lysis labs (LDH, uric acid, phosphate, calcium), lactate, CK, or cardiac biomarkers such as troponin/NT-proBNP.
chemistry.content: Chemistry: Na 135 mmol/L, K 5.3 mmol/L, creatinine 2.4 mg/dL, BUN 25 mg/dL, AST 132 U/L, ALT 163 U/L, bilirubin 2.4 mg/dL, LDH 1289 U/L, uric acid 12.9 mg/dL, Ca²⁺ 2.65 mmol/L, phosphate 4.2 mg/dL, lactate 4.3 mmol/L, CK 120 U/L, Troponin T 0.002 ng/mL, NT-proBNP 4612 pg/mL.

ultrasound_abdomen.valid_request_if: Requests for abdominal ultrasound/sonography, right-upper-quadrant ultrasound, hepatosplenic imaging, or non-cardiac ultrasound when abdominal organs are implied.
ultrasound_abdomen.content: Abdominal ultrasound: Hepatomegaly (18cm) and splenomegaly (15cm); no lymphadenopathy. Horseshoe kidney.

echocardiography.valid_request_if: Requests for transthoracic echocardiogram (TTE), bedside cardiac ultrasound, structural/valvular assessment, or general echocardiography.
echocardiography.content: Echocardiogram: Mitral valve prolapse, tricuspid regurgitation II/III°, EF 54%. TAPSE 10 mm, FAC 30%.

ct_chest.valid_request_if: Requests for CT chest/thorax, CT pulmonary angiogram (CTPA), CT PE study, or unspecified CT imaging when pulmonary embolism or thoracic pathology is implied.
ct_chest.content: CT scan revealed a pulmonary segmental embolism in the right lower lobe, with partial occlusion of segmental branches. No central emboli were identified. The surrounding lung parenchyma showed no infarction or consolidation, and there was no pleural effusion or mediastinal lymphadenopathy.

bone_marrow.valid_request_if: Requests for bone marrow aspirate/biopsy morphology, microscopic marrow description, blast percentage from marrow, or hematopathology narrative (excludes flow or cytogenetics).
bone_marrow.content: On repeat bone marrow examination, morphology revealed hypercellular bone marrow with diffuse infiltration by myeloid blasts (50%) and monocytic blasts (40%) with Auer rods. The blasts showed dysmorphic nuclei with nucleoli and occasional nuclear shadows. Residual hematopoiesis was markedly reduced but showed normal maturation.

flow_cytometry.valid_request_if: Requests for flow cytometry, leukemia immunophenotyping, surface marker profile, CD marker expression, or "flow" shorthand.
flow_cytometry.content: Flow cytometry confirmed a blast population expressing CD34, CD117, HLA-DR, MPO, CD11b, CD13; but negative for CD33, CD7, CD56, TdT. In addition, a monocytic CD14+/CD64+ population with aberrant CD56 expression was detected.

cytogenetics.valid_request_if: Requests for cytogenetics, conventional karyotype, chromosome analysis, or metaphase banding results.

infectious_workup.valid_request_if: Requests for infectious screening, microbiology cultures, colonization surveillance (e.g., ESBL/KPC/MRSA/VRE), inflammatory markers such as CRP or procalcitonin, or virology swabs.
infectious_workup.content: Infectious screening demonstrated an elevated CRP of 32 mg/L, PCT 4.1 ng/mL, while blood cultures and serum galactomannan were negative. A nasopharyngeal swab was positive for enterovirus and rhinovirus. Surveillance rectal/skin swabs revealed ESBL-producing Klebsiella pneumoniae which was KPC positive. VRE and MRSA were negative.

tee_report.valid_request_if: Requests for transesophageal echocardiogram (TEE) results, endocarditis assessment, or valve imaging beyond the transthoracic echo.
tee_report.content: TEE showed no evidence of tricuspid or other valvular endocarditis. Valves otherwise unremarkable. Left ventricular systolic function preserved (EF 53%). Right ventricular function mildly reduced. Moderate tricuspid regurgitation (II/III°).

molecular_panel.valid_request_if: Requests for molecular testing/NGS panels, mutation analysis (e.g., FLT3, NPM1, IDH1/2, CEBPA), or broad gene profiling.
molecular_panel.content: Molecular analysis demonstrated an FLT3-ITD mutation with an allelic ratio of 1.32. No changes were seen in NPM1, IDH1/2, CEBPA,CBFB-MYH11 or RUNX1–RUNX1T1. Likewise, no changes were found in ASXL1, BCOR, EZH2, RUNX1, SF3B1, SRSF2, STAG2, U2AF1, ZRSR2, TP53, DNMT3A, TET2, WT1, NRAS, KRAS, KIT, PTPN11. No KMT2A rearrangements, inv(3)/MECOM or NUP98 fusions.

mrd_status.valid_request_if: Requests for measurable residual disease (MRD) assessments, MRD flow cytometry, molecular MRD markers, or questions such as "Is MRD positive?"
mrd_status.content: Flow cytometry positive (positive:  CD34, CD117, HLA-DR, MPO, CD11b, CD13; negative: CD33, CD7, CD56, TdT). FLT3-ITD mutation.

treatment_history.valid_request_if: Requests for prior therapies, treatment timeline (chemo, targeted agents, prophylaxis), complications during earlier admissions, or how the patient responded to previous regimens.
treatment_history.content: Diagnosis of AML with other defined genetic alterations (WHO 2022) with FLT3-ITD was made on 2024-12-24. 2024-12-25: Hydroxyurea (2 g BID for 4 days, total 8 g) was given for cytoreduction and TLS prophylaxis. 2024-12-29: Induction with “7+3” regimen (cytarabine 100 mg/m²/day × 7 days, total 1,442 mg, and daunorubicin 60 mg/m² × 3 days, total 371 mg) plus quizartinib 35.4 mg once daily from day 8 for 14 days (total 496 mg). Micafungin 50mg/day was initiated as antifungal prophylaxis due to potential CYP450 3A4 interaction. Complications: On 2025-01-05 the patient developed neutropenic fever with pneumonia, treated with intravenous piperacillin/tazobactam 4 g/0.5 g QID for 3 days, followed by meropenem 1 g TID for 11 days. No bloodstream infection was detected. Vancomycin was added for 5 days (1 g BID with TDM, escalated to 1.5 g BID after day 2). Hematopoietic recovery occurred on 2025-01-25 with leukocytes >1,000/µL and platelets >20 × 10⁹/L. Response: Bone marrow assessment on 2025-01-25 showed complete remission with incomplete count recovery (CRi); MRD was not tested. A matched unrelated donor was identified in case the patient would not reach MRD negativity. Induction Cycle 2 (2025-02-02): Cytarabine 100 mg/m²/day × 7 days (total 1,442 mg) plus daunorubicin 45 mg/m²/day (total 278 mg). Quizartinib was administered for 9 days (total 318.6 mg). Complications: On 2025-02-25, septic shock occurred requiring ICU transfer. Blood cultures grew 4MRGN Klebsiella pneumoniae. The patient was treated with ceftazidime/avibactam 2 g/0.5 g TID for 10 days. He developed acute kidney failure (creatinine 3.8 mg/dL), required invasive ventilation, and needed noradrenaline (up to 4 µg/kg/min). Fungal pneumonia was treated with liposomal amphotericin B 400 mg/day (4–5 mg/kg). Hemodialysis was performed until renal function improved. Due to renal failure, liposomal amphotericin B was switched to isavuconazole 200 mg TID for 2 days, followed by 200 mg daily. Right ventricular failure developed during the ICU stay, a CT showed a small pulmonary sub-segmental embolism. After 3 weeks in intensive care, the patient was transferred out of ICU on 2025-03-15. Hematopoietic recovery was documented on 2025-03-09 with leukocytes >1,000/µL and platelets >20 × 10⁹/L. Bone marrow examination on 2025-03-17 confirmed complete remission (CR), FLT3-ITD negative and MRD negativity by flow cytometry. The patient was discharged on 2025-03-23 but was lost to follow-up thereafter. The patient did not proceed with the suggested consolidation cycles and no maintenance therapy was administered.

disease_status.valid_request_if: Requests for the current disease status, response/remission vs relapse classification, or a concise summary of where the leukemia stands now.

special_considerations.valid_request_if: Requests for active issues that influence therapy (organ dysfunction, infections, compliance, clotting/bleeding risks) or any special considerations/contraindications for treatment planning.
special_considerations.content: Worsening tricuspid valve dysfunction (grade II/III). Local abscess at the left arm, suspicious for relapse of prior intravenous drug use. Reduced compliance, history of treatment discontinuation. Prior anthracycline exposure, now with reduced right ventricular ejection fraction. Pulmonary embolism, likely related to prolonged immobility or iv drug abuse. Due to severe thrombocytopenia, no lysis or anticoagulation can be initiated, patient is started on low-dose heparin (100 IE/h). Functional status: ECOG 1–2, deterioration attributed to disease. Active infection requiring treatment.

treatment_plan.action: Hydration and treatment of hyperuricemia (tumor lysis prophylaxis)
Cytoreduction with hydroxyurea (start with 2-3g BID)
Complete vitals, labs, and cultures
Platelet transfusion to >50×10⁹/L and effective anticoagulation for pulmonary embolism
Antiinfective prophylaxis
Bone marrow biopsy for cytology, immunophenotyping, cytogenetics, and molecular genetics
TEE to rule out endocarditis given IV drug use history and tricuspid regurgitation
Admission to hematology ward

key_findings_quotes: history of intravenous drug use, ex-smoker (50 py)
scattered petechiae on the legs, hematomas
Hb 8.3 g/dL, WBC 104 ×10⁹/L, Plt 12 ×10⁹/L
K 5.3 mmol/L, creatinine 2.4 mg/dL, LDH 1289 U/L, uric acid 12.9 mg/dL
BP 98/60, HR 112/min, Respiratory rate: 21/min
Reduced right ventricular function, tricuspid regurgitation II/III°
pulmonary segmental embolism
neurological status was unremarkable

round_2.working_diagnosis: AML with myelomonocytic differentiation; Pulmonary embolism

treatment_plan.action: Complete diagnostics (AML panel molecular genetics including FLT3, FISH for PML::RARA and BCR::ABL1)
Continue cytoreduction with hydroxyurea under hydration and tumor lysis prophylaxis
Antiinfective prophylaxis (antifungal, antibiotic, and PCP)
Hematology board consent for diagnosis and therapy recommendation
HLA-typing of patient and possibly siblings
Cardiology consult prior to induction therapy
Lung function test
Andrology consult for cryopreservation

key_findings_quotes: diffuse infiltration by myeloid blasts (50%) and monocytic blasts (40%) with Auer rods
expressing CD34, CD117, HLA-DR, MPO, CD11b, CD13; but negative for CD33, CD7, CD56, TdT
monocytic CD14+/CD64+ population with aberrant CD56 expression
EF 53%
TEE showed no evidence of tricuspid or other valvular endocarditis

round_3.working_diagnosis: Relapsed AML with other defined genetic alterations (WHO 2022); Pulmonary embolism; Right ventricular heart failure

treatment_plan.action: Salvage therapy with venetoclax (70mg/d for 14 days due to posaconazole comedication) plus HAM (cytarabine 1500 mg/m2 BID over 3 days + mitoxantrone 10 mg/m2 IV QD over 3 days) per RELAX trial protocol
Aim for consolidative allogeneic HCT from matched unrelated donor (MUD) if CR2 achieved, with busulfan/cyclophosphamide/ATG conditioning and FLT3 inhibitor maintenance
Platelet transfusion to >50×10⁹/L and effective anticoagulation for PE; monitor closely and increase heparin dose once platelets recover
Surgical incision on left arm abscess with antibiotic treatment (linezolid 600 mg BID plus piperacillin/tazobactam 4.5g q6h), samples for microbiology to rule out ESBL-producing organisms
TEE to evaluate worsening tricuspid dysfunction and rule out endocarditis
Psychiatric consult for close co-management given adherence concerns

key_findings_quotes: FLT3-ITD mutation with an allelic ratio of 1.32
FLT3-ITD negative and MRD negativity by flow cytometry after cycle 2, then lost to follow-up
reduced right ventricular ejection fraction
Active infection requiring treatment
Pulmonary embolism, severe thrombocytopenia
matched unrelated donor was identified
Worsening tricuspid valve dysfunction (grade II/III)
Local abscess at the left arm, suspicious for relapse of prior intravenous drug use
